Keep the phone steady when asked, and move it in the requested pattern only during the measuring step. Step 1/3: Keep device still for gyro Step 2/3: Move in figure‑8 Step 3/3: Place on flat surface Lay the phone flat on a table and keep your hands off it while the gyroscope bias is measured. Rotate the phone smoothly in a wide figure‑8 pattern so the magnetometer can see all directions. Place the phone flat again and keep it still so the accelerometer can calibrate gravity. Tap Start Guided Calibration to begin. The app will show each step and update the progress bar. Start Guided Calibration Skip Magnetometer Step Use this if your device does not have a magnetometer. Calibration Guide Sensor Info Connection Guide About Flat Up Title Place device on a flat surface facing up Collection Progress: %d%% Stability: %.2f Rotate device slowly along each axis Variance: %.4f Gyroscope ready. Click start to continue. Move device in a figure-8 pattern Magnetometer calibrated. Ready to proceed. Use two fingers to scroll, one finger to tap License ... Quick Start

1. Install and run the Air Mouse server on your PC.
2. Make sure both devices are connected to the same Wi‑Fi network.
3. Enter the PC IP address or scan the QR code from the server.
4. Calibrate the sensors before the first session.
5. Tap Start Air Mouse and adjust sensitivity if needed.

Air Mouse Controls

• Move cursor: Rotate the phone smoothly to move the pointer.
• Left click: Perform a short quick flick gesture.
• Right click: Tilt the phone to the configured right-click angle.
• Double click: Perform two quick click gestures in succession.
• Scroll up/down: Move the phone vertically with a faster, shorter motion.

Practical Tips
• Keep the phone steady during calibration and while not actively moving the cursor.
• Start with medium sensitivity, then increase it only if the pointer feels slow.
• If gestures feel delayed, check Wi‑Fi quality and keep the phone close to the PC.
• Use the drawer to open calibration, statistics, logs, and the advanced tools. Why Calibration Matters

Calibration removes sensor drift and makes cursor movement, clicks, and scrolling more reliable.

1. Gyroscope Calibration
Place the phone flat on a stable table and do not touch it while the app measures the bias offset.

2. Magnetometer Calibration
Slowly move the phone through a wide figure‑8 pattern so it can sample the surrounding magnetic field from multiple angles.

3. Accelerometer Calibration
Put the phone flat again and keep it still so the app can capture the gravity reference used for motion detection.

4. Completion
When the progress bar reaches 100%, return to the Home screen and press Start. If the room has strong magnetic noise, repeat calibration once more. Device Sensors Used

• Gyroscope (3‑axis): Tracks angular velocity and powers the cursor movement and click gestures.
• Accelerometer (3‑axis): Detects linear motion, gravity direction, and scroll gestures.
• Magnetometer (3‑axis): Improves heading stability and reduces long-term drift when available.

How the app uses them
The app combines sensor streams with a Madgwick filter and gesture detectors so the cursor remains responsive while still filtering noise and tremor.

Missing sensors?
The app keeps working even if a sensor is not available, but some features will be simplified or disabled to preserve stability. How to Connect to PC

1. On your PC
• Start the Air Mouse server application.
• Keep the server window open so it can accept connections.
• Copy the local IP address shown by the server or encode it in the QR code.

2. On your phone
• Connect to the same Wi‑Fi network as the PC.
• Enter the IP address manually or use the QR scanner to fill it automatically.
• Tap Start Air Mouse after calibration is complete.

Troubleshooting
• Verify that firewall rules allow the server port used by the desktop app.
• Make sure both devices are on the same subnet and that VPN is disabled while testing.
• If the connection fails, re-check the IP address and restart the server once. Air Mouse Pro
Version %s

Turn your Android phone into a wireless air mouse using motion sensors, gesture recognition, and guided setup screens that are built for a complete end-to-end experience.

Developed by: Arian Firoozi, Arsalan Talaee
Instructors: Dr. Mohsen Shokri, Dr. Mehdi Kargahi
University of Tehran
Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering
Second Semester 1404-1405 (2025‑2026)

Try again. Gyroscope not available No custom gestures saved yet. Server Log Edge Gestures Enable floating action button Tap = click, long press = right click Welcome to Air Mouse Pro Turn your phone into a wireless air mouse using motion sensors. Intuitive Gestures Rotate, tilt, and move naturally to control your PC. Quick Connection Scan a QR code or enter IP address to connect instantly. Advanced Features Voice commands, custom gestures, statistics, and more. Next Skip setup Start Air Mouse Information Version %s What is Air Mouse Pro? Air Mouse Pro turns your phone into a wireless air mouse. Use natural hand movements to control your PC cursor, click, scroll, and more – no physical mouse needed. App Version Credits Developed by: Arian Firoozi, Arsalan Talaee Instructors: Dr. Mohsen Shokri, Dr. Mehdi Kargahi University of Tehran – Faculty of Electrical and Computer Engineering Technology • Madgwick AHRS sensor fusion\n• Material 3 design with dynamic theming\n• QR code connection\n• Voice commands & custom gestures\n• Real‑time statistics\n• Low‑latency UDP/TCP communication Open Source License This project is released under the MIT License.
